title: Behavioral Guidelines
use_when: Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring code to avoid overcomplication, make surgical changes, surface assumptions, and define verifiable success criteria.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Behavioral Guidelines when the request is mainly metric diagnosis, pricing, GTM, or implementation planning without UX evidence; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Behavioral Guidelines UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Behavioral Guidelines fits Design work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Behavioral Guidelines UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Behavioral Guidelines UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Behavioral Guidelines as generic Design advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Behavioral Guidelines when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $behavioral-guidelines to turn this behavioral context into a decision-ready Behavioral Guidelines UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Behavioral Guidelines UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
